correct_explanation: long-scoreboard stalls dominate because the kernel spends most of its time waiting on dependent operations rather than saturating bandwidth
raw_response:
BOTTLENECK: latency-bound

REASONING: The kernel is using almost none of the available memory bandwidth (0.05% of peak) and only a tiny fraction of compute throughput (2.88% of peak), so it is not saturating either the memory system or the arithmetic units. With high achieved occupancy (87.07%), the GPU has plenty of resident warps, yet performance remains low, which points to stalls from waiting on operations rather than lack of parallelism; the nontrivial long-scoreboard stall rate is consistent with latency-limited execution.
parsed_reasoning: The kernel is using almost none of the available memory bandwidth (0.05% of peak) and only a tiny fraction of compute throughput (2.88% of peak), so it is not saturating either the memory system or the arithmetic units. With high achieved occupancy (87.07%), the GPU has plenty of resident warps, yet performance remains low, which points to stalls from waiting on operations rather than lack of parallelism; the nontrivial long-scoreboard stall rate is consistent with latency-limited execution.

prompt_rendered.user:
Diagnose the primary performance bottleneck 
in this CUDA kernel:

Without seeing the kernel code, given only these metrics:
- Achieved memory bandwidth: 0.05% of peak
- Achieved FLOPS: 2.88% of peak
- Warp stall (long scoreboard): 13.6%
- Achieved occupancy: 87.07%

What is the primary bottleneck?
